message:
style: clear remaining clippy backlog across cli/harness/wasm

Last clippy cleanup pass after the column-name inference unification
and pragma_maintenance / collapsible_match commits earlier in this
batch.  All five files were carrying lints that the unified lint
profile started flagging once the rest of the workspace was clean.

crates/fsqlite-cli/src/main.rs

- ShellOptions::forced_batch: tag with
  #[allow(clippy::unused_self)] and an inline comment "signature
  parallels nested_script for symmetry".  forced_batch and
  nested_script return ShellOptions and intentionally have the
  same `(self) -> Self` shape so callers can chain them
  uniformly; making forced_batch a free function would force
  all the call sites to be asymmetric.
- sql_segment_has_tokens: collapse the four parallel
  StatementScanState arms (SingleQuote / DoubleQuote / Backtick
  / BracketIdent → unreachable!()) into a single `|`-separated
  arm.  Same control flow, fewer lines, satisfies
  clippy::match_same_arms.
- try_execute_dot_command: tag with
  #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)].  The function takes
  trimmed, connection, plus the six-piece dot-command execution
  context (writers, env, history, prompt state, ...).  Bundling
  these into an Args struct would force every dot-command call
  site through an extra .build() step for no real readability
  win.

crates/fsqlite-harness/src/bloodstream.rs

- File-level
  `#![allow(clippy::mutable_key_type)]`
  with the same explanatory comment used in connection.rs:
  SmallText carries a OnceLock for lazy long-string interning,
  which trips mutable_key_type at every BTreeMap<SmallText, _>
  site even though hash and equality remain stable.  Bloodstream
  is the second consumer (after fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs)
  to need this allow.

crates/fsqlite-harness/src/certification_policy.rs

- certification_gate_config(): rewrite as a struct literal with
  ..GateConfig::default() instead of the
    let mut config = GateConfig::default();
    config.release_threshold = 1.0;
    config.category_min_verification_pct = ...;
    config
  pattern.  Functionally identical, more idiomatic, satisfies
  clippy::field_reassign_with_default.

crates/fsqlite-harness/src/release_certificate.rs

- Four call sites of `if let Some(false) = expr { ... }` —
  one in build_certificate (artifact_manifest_gate_passed),
  one in build_certificate (final_gate_passed), and the same
  pair in determine_verdict — rewritten as
  `if expr == Some(false) { ... }`.  Same semantics, satisfies
  clippy::redundant_pattern_matching and reads as the comparison
  it actually is.

crates/fsqlite-wasm/src/lib.rs

- WasmDatabaseOptions::memory_vfs_config: tag with
  #[allow(clippy::useless_let_if_seq)] and an inline comment
  "three independent optional fields".  The function builds a
  MemoryVfsConfig by sequentially `if let Some(...) =
  self.<field>` over three independent options.  Clippy wants us
  to fold them into one match, which would force the three
  branches to share a tuple destructure even though they're
  independently configurable.  The allow is the right call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
